prs.title: fix: NoReverseMatch error in checkin lists template
prs.body: Fixes #2291 How I fixed it Updated the template to use the correct URL pattern name that actually exists in the codebase: * Changed the URL from `"control:event.item"` to `"control:event.product"` * Updated the parameter from `item=item.id` to `product=item.id` to match what the URL expects Testing done I've tested this locally and confirmed: * Check-in lists page now loads without errors when lists have limited products * Products linked to check-in lists are displayed correctly with working links * Product links navigate to the correct product page * The check-in lists display all information properly Screenshots Before: After: ## Summary by Sourcery Bug Fixes: - Resolve NoReverseMatch errors on the check-in lists page by linking limited products to an existing product route. ## Summary by Sourcery Bug Fixes: - Resolve NoReverseMatch errors on the check-in lists page when rendering limited products linked to a check-in list.

prs.title: fix:NoReverseMatch error in question delete template
prs.body: Fixes #1441 How I fixed it Updated the template to use the correct URL pattern name that actually exists in the codebase: * Changed the URL from `"control:event.item"` to `"control:event.product"` * Updated the parameter from `item=item.pk` to `product=item.pk` to match what the URL expects Testing done I've tested this locally and confirmed: * The question delete page now loads without errors * Products linked to the question are displayed correctly with working links * The delete confirmation shows the proper warning message about lost answers * Both Cancel and Delete buttons work as expected Before: After: ## Summary by Sourcery Bug Fixes: - Correct the URL name and parameter in the question delete template so dependent product links resolve without NoReverseMatch errors. ## Summary by Sourcery Bug Fixes: - Correct the URL name and parameter in the question delete template so dependent product links point to the existing event product route.

prs.title: fix: NoReverseMatch error in quota delete template
prs.body: Fixes #1404 How I fixed it Updated the template to use the correct URL pattern name that actually exists in the codebase: - Changed the URL from `"control:event.item"` to `"control:event.product"` - Updated the parameter from `item=item.pk` to `product=item.pk` to match what the URL expects Testing done I've tested this locally and confirmed: - Deleting quotas without products works fine - Deleting quotas with products now shows the proper confirmation page - The Cancel button takes you back to the quotas list as expected ## Summary by Sourcery Bug Fixes: - Resolve NoReverseMatch errors on the quota deletion page by using the existing product URL pattern and parameter.

prs.title: docs: add National Policy on Disaster Management 2009 guide
prs.body:
## Description
This PR adds a comprehensive study guide on the National Policy on Disaster Management 2009 for Module 5, covering all required aspects as per issue #9.

## Related Issue
Closes #9

## Changes Made
- ✅ Added comprehensive guide (2800+ words) on National Policy on Disaster Management 2009
- ✅ Covered all 9 key objectives with detailed explanations
- ✅ Documented 10+ salient features comprehensively
- ✅ Explained paradigm shift from relief to prevention
- ✅ Outlined institutional mechanisms (NDMA, SDMA, DDMA)
- ✅ Detailed NDRF role, structure, and achievements
- ✅ Included implementation framework
- ✅ Documented achievements since implementation
- ✅ Added Sendai Framework alignment section
- ✅ Updated contributors table in README

## Content Highlights
- Background and context of NPDM 2009
- Vision statement analysis
- Comprehensive coverage of objectives and features
- Institutional framework at all levels
- NDRF's role with specific achievements
- Real-world implementation examples
- International alignment with Sendai Framework

## Checklist
- [x] File created at correct location: `modules/module-5/national-disaster-management-policy-guide.md`
- [x] Content exceeds minimum word count (2800+ words vs 700-900 required)
- [x] All required sections covered
- [x] Proper markdown formatting
- [x] Updated README.md contributors table
- [x] Follows repository guidelines

prs.title: Add link to main OpsiMate website in navbar and footer
prs.body:

I've added the website link in two places:

This makes it easy for users to find the main website while reading the docs, and also helps with SEO and navigation between the main site and documentation.

prs.title: Add smooth transitions to form elements
prs.body:
## Changes
This PR adds smooth CSS transitions to form elements for a better user experience.

### Improvements made:
- Added smooth color transitions to buttons on hover (0.2s ease)
- Added smooth transform effect when buttons are pressed
- Added smooth box-shadow transitions for form inputs on focus
- Improved disabled state styling with grayscale filter for better visual distinction
- Implemented `focus-visible` for better keyboard navigation accessibility (focus rings only show when using keyboard, not mouse clicks)

### Testing
Tested on localhost:3000 with:
- Button hover effects ✓
- Button click animations ✓
- Input field focus transitions ✓
- Disabled input styling ✓

prs.title: Improve CSS: enhanced scrollbars and button hover effects
prs.body:
## Changes Made

This PR improves the visual design and user experience of the documentation site:

### CSS Improvements:
- **Enhanced scrollbars**: Increased width from 4px to 8px for better usability, added smooth hover transitions, and made scrollbars consistently styled across the entire site
- **Smoother content transitions**: Added opacity transitions for more polished page loading
- **Better button interactions**: Added scale and glow effects on hover for cover page buttons

### Benefits:
- More modern and polished appearance
- Better user experience with easier-to-grab scrollbars
- Smoother, more engaging interactions throughout the site

All changes are backwards compatible and maintain the existing dark theme aesthetic.

prs.title: Add like button feature to blog posts
prs.body:
## Description
Implemented a like button feature for blog posts as requested in issue #58.

## Features Implemented
✅ Like button with heart icon appears below each blog post  
✅ Users can give up to 10 likes per article (like Hashnode)  
✅ Like count displays and updates in real-time  
✅ "Remove one" button to decrease likes  
✅ "Maxed out!" indicator when reaching 10 likes  
✅ Likes persist using localStorage (no login required)

## Testing
Tested locally and confirmed all features work correctly including:
- Adding likes up to maximum of 10
- Removing likes
- Persistence after page refresh

prs.title: my first pr

issues.title: NoReverseMatch error when navigating check-in lists
issues.body: **Describe the bug** When trying to open check-in-lists, we get an error page with a NoReverseMatch error like this: Expected behavior The check-in list details page should display, showing: - Check-in list information - List of limited products (if any) - All check-in list settings and options Steps to reproduce - Navigate to Check-in Lists page

issues.title: Error page when trying to delete a question tied to products
issues.body: Describe the bug When trying to delete a question that is attached to products, we get an error page with a `NoReverseMatch` error like this: Expected behavior A proper delete confirmation page should load, showing: - A warning message about the question being deleted - A list of products that are linked to this question with clickable links - Cancel and Delete buttons to proceed or abort the deletion Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new question 2. Link the question to one or more products 3. Try to delete the question 4. Error page appears instead of the delete confirmation page Additional context This bug completely blocks users from deleting questions that are linked to products, forcing them to manually unlink all products first before deletion. . This is similar to issue #1404 but occurs in a different template file (questions vs quotas).
